issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed the code only adds hidden to the overlay; the drawer element itself is never given aria‑hidden="true", so it remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button receives aria‑haspopup but never receives an aria‑expanded state when the panel is opened or closed, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s visibility.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed the code sets hidden on the panel but does not set aria‑hidden="true", so the panel stays in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator adds error messages and CSS classes but never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the field when validation fails, nor removes it when the field becomes valid. Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey the error state.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, sorting, or paginating products the UI updates the count badge and grid, but no aria‑live announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new result set.
issuesFound.fix:
After the animation completes, add:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches!==1?'s':''} shown`);
```

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not update aria‑pressed or announce change

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user selects a new billing period the UI updates prices, but no aria‑live announcement is made, so screen‑reader users cannot hear the change.
issuesFound.fix:


issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions lack live‑region announcements

issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: Elements hidden by productFilter animation are not given aria‑hidden

issuesFound.fix:
In the hide‑phase of filterAnimation.run add:  
```js
toHide.forEach(el => el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'));
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never set or update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.open` set `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true')`; in `mobileNav.close` set `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button (`.nav-toggle`) does not have an `aria-controls` attribute that references the ID of the drawer element, breaking the relationship between the control and the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: Assign a unique ID to the drawer (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`) and add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls','mobile-nav-drawer')` during initialization.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, the code only removes CSS classes; it never adds `aria-hidden="true"` (or the `hidden` attribute) to the drawer element, so the hidden navigation remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.close` add `drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')`; in `mobileNav.open` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance` toggles the dropdown panel’s visibility but never updates `aria-expanded` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the open state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance` hides the dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Add `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels not hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is closed the code only adds a CSS class and later sets `hidden` after a transition, but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` immediately, so the panel remains perceivable to assistive technologies during the transition.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance` set `panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')` as soon as the close begins; remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` method adds the open class and animates the panel but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add `item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true')` when opening.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate` routine marks fields visually but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to inputs that fail validation, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: When a field is invalid, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid','true')`; when valid, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid on success
issuesFound.explanation: After a field becomes valid, the validator removes visual error styling but does not remove `aria-invalid`, leaving the field incorrectly announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: On successful validation, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge` changes the numeric count of visible products but does not announce the change to a screen‑reader via an ARIA live region, so users are unaware of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge, set `statusEl.textContent = `${count} product${count!==1?'s':''} found`;` and ensure `statusEl` has `aria-live="polite"` (or reuse the existing live region).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce updated price
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod` updates price numbers on all plan cards but does not provide an audible announcement of the new pricing, leaving users who cannot see the numbers uninformed.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call `liveRegion.announce('Pricing switched to '+BILLING_PERIODS[period].label+' billing');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened or closed, no ARIA live region is updated, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback about the state change.
issuesFound.fix: In `open` and `close`, after changing state, call `liveRegion.announce(item.question + (item.open ? ' expanded' : ' collapsed'));`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown does not trigger any live‑region update, so users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer does not announce the state change to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close`, add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu '+(open?'opened':'closed'));`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button for billing period lacks aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period switch (`[data-billing-toggle]`) acts as a binary toggle but never updates `aria-pressed` (or `aria-checked` for a switch), making its state invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `pricing.setPeriod`, after changing period, call `toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-pressed', period==='year' ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button for view mode lacks aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (`[data-view-mode]`) toggle between grid and list but never set `aria-pressed`, so the current mode is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, set `button.setAttribute('aria-pressed', button.getAttribute('data-view-mode')===currentMode ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels not given `aria-hidden` when hidden (duplicate of Issue 6, but separate function)
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function hides the panel but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel element, leaving it exposed to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Add `panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')` in the close routine.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing `role="dialog"` or `role="navigation"` with appropriate ARIA labeling
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element lacks a landmark role, so screen‑reader users cannot quickly locate the navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Add `drawer.setAttribute('role','navigation')` (or `dialog` if appropriate) and give it an accessible name via `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack `hidden` attribute when collapsed (reliance on CSS only)
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` method only animates height and does not set the native `hidden` attribute, which is required for reliable hiding from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After the collapse animation completes, call `panel.setAttribute('hidden','');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons missing explicit `type="button"`
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons used to open dropdowns are not given `type="button"`, causing them to act as submit buttons in forms, which can lead to unexpected form submissions.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each dropdown trigger includes `type="button"` in its markup or set it via JavaScript during initialization.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` in the dropdown menu controller change the visibility of the panel (add/remove the “open” class and hidden attribute) but never set or update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu visibility changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing a dropdown updates the DOM but does not write a message to any element with `role="status"`/`aria-live`. Users of assistive technology receive no audible cue that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `aria-expanded`, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` (or similar) in `_openInstance` and `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')` in `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In the FAQ controller the `open(item)` function expands a panel but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, and `close(item)` never sets it to `"false"`. This violates the disclosure pattern required for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel hidden state missing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ panel is closed the code adds the `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel element, so the hidden content may still be exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In `close(item)` after `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` also call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open(item)` remove the attribute with `item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item updates the UI but does not provide an audible announcement. Users are not informed which question was opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `aria-expanded`, call `liveRegion.announce('Answer expanded for “' + item.question + '”')` in `open(item)` and a corresponding “collapsed” message in `close(item)`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates price text but does not send a live‑region announcement, leaving users unaware of the new pricing context.
issuesFound.fix: After `setPeriod` updates the UI, add `liveRegion.announce('Billing period switched to ' + periodLabel(period))`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count badge is updated, but no live‑region message is emitted, so users of assistive technology do not know how many products match the filter.
issuesFound.fix: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches)`, call `liveRegion.announce(totalMatches + ' product' + (totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : '') + ' shown')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer updates classes and focus but does not provide an audible cue via a live region, so screen‑reader users are not informed of the navigation state change.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` after toggling, add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened')`; in `close()` add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which renders no visible focus ring because the outline width is zero. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see which interactive element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline entirely (outline: none) and provides only outline-offset. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, so users receive no visible focus indicator at all.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button

While the :focus state has a box-shadow, :focus-visible specifically removes all indicators, leaving keyboard users without a visible focus ring in forced-colors mode where box-shadow is suppressed.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image hover

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function displays the mobile navigation drawer by adding CSS classes but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state of the menu to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1163 (inside open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the mobile navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1178 (inside close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1253 (inside _openInstance function): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the dropdown panel after a CSS transition but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "false", leaving screen readers thinking the menu is still open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1270 (inside _closeInstance function): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening the search suggestions panel, the function sets internal state but does not update the combobox input's aria-expanded attribute to "true", preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2103 (inside _open function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the search suggestions, the function clears state and hides the panel but leaves aria-expanded undefined or stale on the input element instead of setting it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2118 (inside _close function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) cycle between active states but the _syncToggleState function only updates CSS classes without setting aria-pressed to indicate which button is currently active/pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2585 (inside _syncToggleState function): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and error messages but never calls setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true') on the input element, so screen readers cannot identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3070 (inside _renderResult else block): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation passes, the function removes error styling but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, potentially leaving screen readers with stale invalid state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3060 (inside _renderResult if block): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization, the mobile navigation trigger button is discovered and event listeners attached, but aria-expanded is never initialized to "false" to reflect the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210 (inside init function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;` and before adding event listeners.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function sets up dropdown triggers with aria-haspopup and aria-controls but never initializes aria-expanded="false", leaving the initial state ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1295 (inside register function): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the line `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element is configured as a combobox but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" during setup, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial closed state of the suggestion panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165 (inside init function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button controls the mobile navigation drawer but does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer's ID, preventing screen readers from identifying the controlled region relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1212 (inside init function): Add `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav-drawer'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);` after ensuring the drawer has an ID.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, keyboard focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or the first focusable element inside it, forcing keyboard users to tab through the entire page to reach the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1170 (inside open function): Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` after adding the open class.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the mobile menu, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, potentially leaving keyboard focus in an unexpected location or causing focus loss.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the drawer via CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element, meaning screen readers may still perceive the off-screen or display:none content as present in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185 (inside close function): Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` when closing (or ensure it is set when the CSS hides the element).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden removal when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function reveals the drawer but does not remove aria-hidden or set it to "false", which may leave the content hidden from screen readers even when visually displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1165 (inside open function): Add `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` or `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');` when opening.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, the prices update visually with animation but no announcement is made to screen readers to inform them of the price change or the selected billing period.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the content becomes visible but screen readers are not notified of the state change unless they manually check the aria-expanded attribute; an explicit announcement improves user awareness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2875 (inside open function): Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));` before emitting the custom event.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is collapsed, the function updates ARIA attributes but does not announce the collapsed state to screen readers, which can leave users uncertain whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2895 (inside close function): Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));` before emitting the custom event.

issuesFound.fix: Line 420: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link in the mobile menu uses a visual class for styling but does not expose aria-current="page", preventing assistive technology users from identifying their current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 425: <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links container is a navigation landmark without an aria-label. When multiple nav elements exist on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this landmark from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 205: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary desktop navigation lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks such as the footer or mobile navigation in screen reader landmark lists.
issuesFound.fix: Line 410: <nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav" aria-label="Desktop">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section element is a significant page region but lacks an accessible name via aria-labelledby or aria-label, reducing navigability for screen reader users browsing by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section contains a heading but the section element lacks aria-labelledby, preventing it from being identified as a labelled landmark region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element containing a heading with an ID, but the article lacks aria-labelledby. Screen reader users browsing by article landmarks cannot identify which plan each card represents.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1000: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The component receives an invalid prop to style error states but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen reader users cannot perceive when the input contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 330: <input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but fails to communicate the invalid state to assistive technology via aria-invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: <textarea id={id} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The select element uses the invalid prop for visual styling but does not expose aria-invalid, preventing screen reader users from detecting validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: <select id={id} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Checkbox missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid to the input element, leaving assistive technology users unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: <input id={id} type="checkbox" aria-describedby={errorId} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className="checkbox-input" {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for visual marquee scrolling is not marked with aria-hidden. Screen reader users will encounter duplicate content when navigating through the integration partners.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides not marked aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Non-visible slides in the carousel remain in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users navigating by headings or landmarks will encounter content from slides that are not currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 990: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is placed on the overlay backdrop instead of the dialog element itself. Screen readers may not treat the dialog as modal, allowing users to navigate outside the active dialog.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1530: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: NotificationContainer suppressing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The container has aria-live="off", which may prevent screen readers from announcing toast notifications automatically. Users will not be alerted to important status messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1590: <div className="notification-container" aria-label="Notifications">
